violations.text: Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2025

Chicken and empanadas were observed held at 105 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Chicken and empanadas were reheated to above 165 DF and placed in a properly functioning hot hold unit. Establishment is not to use the heat lamp hot-hold unit due to it not being able to keep food 135 DF or above. Per PIC, a new unit will be purchased. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2025

Containers of dough and boxes of cheese were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Establishment has addition storage racks that will be placed in walk-in cooler by the end of day. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the small prep unit have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Soap was provided at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Paper towels were added at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Dishes were removed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils can opener and vegetable chopper were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener and vegetable chopper were moved to 3-bay sink at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

Fried chicken was observed held at 120 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Fried chicken was reheated to above 165 DF at the time of inspection. Establishment is not to store any hot foods in hold unit if it is unable to keep the food at 135 DF or higher. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

Chicken wings were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per PIC, food thermometer is on order. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025
Beef was observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025
The sanitizer dispenser for the 3-bay sink was observed not working properly, leaking, and in poor repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2025

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Establishment is to obtain test strips. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in cooler shelves, chest freezer, and both prep units have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025

The hand washing sink by the food preparation area was observed with no running water. All hand washing sinks must be provided with running water. Establishment is to service hand sink. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

The floor in the back storage area and behind grill line has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025
The walk-in cooler light was observed not working and in poor repair. Establishment is to repair lighting in walk-in cooler. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Soap was provided at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Paper towels were provided at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025

Fried chicken and beef patties were observed held at 110 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was re-heated to above 165 at the time of inspection. Establishment is to monitor food temperatures in hold-hold unit to make sure food is able to be kept at 135 DF or higher. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025

Chicken wings were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per conversation with owner over phone, thermometer will be purchased and brought in by end of day. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients/flour are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025
Beef and chicken were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025
The sanitizer dispenser for the 3-bay sink was observed not working properly, leaking, and in poor repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Establishment is to obtain test strips. New Violation. Correct By: 12/05/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in cooler shelves, chest freezer, and both prep units have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025

The hand washing sink by the food preparation area was observed with no running water. All hand washing sinks must be provided with running water. Establishment is to service hand sink. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

The floor in the back storage area and behind grill line has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025
The walk-in cooler light was observed not working and in poor repair. Establishment is to repair lighting in walk-in cooler. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025

Lids and empty cheese package were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

Chicken was observed held at (93) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

Working containers of (dry food product) are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the food storage shelves have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
The prep sink in the kitchen area is in poor repair. Observed a leak at the pipes under prep sink. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2024

The floors and walls in walk in cooler have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
The floor and wall junctures in the walk-in cooler do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2023
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Sponges, soap dispenser and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
At the time of inspection handwash sink was lacking signage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
Chicken and cilantro, located in the walk-in cooler, was observed adulterated. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Raw animal foods (chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (various sauces) in the walk-in refrigerator, 1 door low boy and prep unit. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (cutting board table, onion slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Various food products were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
Inspector observed employees' medicine stored with sauces and were not stored to prevent the contamination of food, equipment, or utensils. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

(Chemical) was stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
Inspector observed rodenticide in back storage area uncovered. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Working containers of flour are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Inspector observed frozen foods in freezer stored uncovered. Food items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
Single service items are not being stored properly and are stored on a surface that is not clean in the cashier area. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the 3 bay sink and handwashing sink have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
Inspector observed 3 bay sink leaking from the faucet. New Violation. Correct By: 09/01/2023
Inspector observed light bulb above pizza oven in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023
Prep unit located in the prep area was not in good repair. (Ambient 48 degrees F.) Service called during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2023
Plumbing to the 3 bay sink is in poor repair, leaks observed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2023
